title: Two Suspects Charged After Pride Flag Burned in Markham
content:
Investigators with the York Regional Police Hate Crime Prevention Unit have charged two suspects after a Pride flag was damaged and set on fire at a secondary school in the City of Markham.
On Tuesday, June 9, 2026, officers responded to a school in the area of Yonge Street and Clark Avenue following reports that a Pride flag had been burned.
The investigation determined that on Monday, June 8, 2026, at approximately 11 p.m., two suspects attended the school, removed the Pride flag from its holder, ripped it and attempted to set it on fire.
Sepehr
ABADAR, 18, of Markham, and Mahan Mohammad
RADMARD, 18, of Markham, have each been charged with Mischief Under $5,000.
Investigators believe the incident was hate-motivated.
York Regional Police takes all hate-motivated incidents seriously and will not tolerate any form of hate crime. All reports of hate crime or hate/bias incidents are investigated thoroughly. Anyone who witnesses or has been the victim of a hate crime or hate/bias incident is encouraged to report it to police immediately.
Anyone with information is asked to contact the York Regional Police Hate Crime Prevention Unit by calling 1-866-876-5423, ext. 6552 or by email at
hatecrime@yrp.ca
. Anonymous tips can be sent to Crime Stoppers by calling 1-800-222-TIPS or by visiting
www.1800222tips.com
.

title: Registered Massage Therapist Facing Sexual Assault Charges
content:
Investigators with the York Regional Police Special Victims Unit have charged a Registered Massage Therapist with two counts of sexual assault, and are seeking additional victims, following an investigation in the towns of Aurora and Newmarket.
On April 8, 2026, the victim attended a massage appointment at a wellness clinic in the area of Yonge Street and Edward Street. During the massage, the suspect began touching the victim for a sexual purpose.
The following day, Augusto
OLIVEIRA CUNHA, 52, of the Town of Newmarket, was charged with one count of sexual assault.
On May 21, 2026, a second victim came forward and reported having been sexually assaulted by the suspect at the same clinic.
The victim had attended a massage appointment on April 4, 2026. During the massage, the suspect sexually assaulted the victim.
OLIVEIRA CUNHA
was charged with an additional count of sexual assault on June 4, 2026.
Investigators are releasing the suspect’s image as they believe there may be additional victims.
He is known to have been employed at several wellness clinics in Aurora and Newmarket.
Charged:
Augusto
OLIVEIRA CUNHA, 52, of the Town of Newmarket
Charges:
Sexual Assault x2
A sexual assault includes any non-consensual contact of a sexual nature. York Regional Police would like to encourage anyone who feels they may have been a victim of a sexual assault to come forward and report the incident to police. There is no statute of limitations for sexual offences and offenders can be prosecuted well after the date of the offence.
If someone is not ready to report, however is seeking support, please contact
York Region Victim Services
or the
Women’s Support Network.
Additional resources and information can be found on our
website.
Anyone with information is asked to contact the York Regional Police Special Victims Unit at 1-866-876-5423, ext. 7071. Anonymous tips can also be made to Crime Stoppers at 1-800-222-TIPS, or by visiting
www.1800222tips.com
.

title: Renewed Public Warning of Suspects Scouting Condos for Break and Enters
content:
York Regional Police is issuing a renewed warning to the public after two incidents of suspects scouting units prior to committing break and enters into condominiums.
Recently, two incidents were reported in Markham and Richmond Hill, involving unidentified suspects surreptitiously gaining entrance into condominium and apartment buildings. The suspects applied a glue-like substance on unit doors throughout the buildings. The suspects returned at a later time to examine the condition of the glue to determine if the units were occupied before committing break and enters.
Owners and tenants should be aware of this crime technique and report any suspicious persons or activities to police and to their condo management or building superintendent.
Video of the suspects can be seen here:
https://youtu.be/Ni1vhxmG2F4
Anyone with information is asked to contact the York Regional Police #5 District Criminal Investigations Bureau at 1-866-876-5423, ext. 7545, or call Crime Stoppers at 1-800-222-TIPS, or leave an anonymous tip online at
www.1800222tips.com
.
Visit our
Community Safety Data Portal
for complete stats and crime data within York Region. Crime prevention is our shared responsibility. Learn more about
Operation Streetview
.

title: Update – One Man Charged, Two Suspects Sought in Markham Carjacking
content:
Investigators with the York Regional Police Hold-Up Unit have charged one man and are seeking two additional suspects following a carjacking which occurred in the City of Markham in March 2026.
On March 3, 2026, at 11:43 p.m., officers responded to reports of a carjacking at Warden Avenue and Highway 407. The victim was stopped at an intersection when the suspect vehicle struck the victim’s vehicle from behind. When the victim exited to assess the damage, he was assaulted by two suspects. The two suspects fled in the victim’s vehicle, with a third suspect operating the suspect vehicle, described as a white sedan.
The victim sustained minor injuries.
The following day, the victim’s vehicle was recovered in the area of Matheson Boulevard East and Orbitor Drive in the City of Mississauga.
Through investigative efforts, one suspect was identified as Waheed
RAHMATI, 22, of no fixed address.
He was charged on June 4, 2026, with Robbery, Disguise with Intent and two counts of Possession of Property Obtained by Crime Over $5,000.
When he was charged,
RAHMATI
was already in custody for unrelated offences.
The two additional suspects were seen wearing dark face coverings and dark clothing at the time of the offence.
The investigation is ongoing.
Anyone with information is asked to contact the York Regional Police Hold-Up Unit at 1-866-876-5423, ext. 6630. Anonymous tips can also be made to Crime Stoppers at 1-800-222-TIPS, or by visiting
www.1800222tips.com
.

title: Video - Automated Licence Plate Reader Leads to Recovery  of Stolen Vehicle and Arrest in Aurora
content:
York Regional Police (YRP) have recovered a stolen vehicle and charged a suspect after an Automated Licence Plate Recognition (ALPR) alert in the Town of Aurora. This incident underscores the growing role of technology in helping police detect crime and protect the community.
On Thursday, June 4, 2026, an officer on patrol in the area of Yonge Street and Elm Grove Avenue received an alert from the in-car ALPR system identifying a grey Ford Edge as being stolen. The officer continued to observe the vehicle while additional units and Air Support Unit moved in to assist.
With the assistance of the YRP Air Support and Canine Units, the suspect was taken into custody at St. John’s Sideroad and Industrial Parkway South.
Kyvam
AGHAKHANI BARFEH, 23, of the City of Barrie has been charged with Theft of Motor Vehicle, Possession of Property Obtained by Crime Over $5,000, Dangerous Operation, two counts of Breach of Probation and three counts of Operation of a Conveyance while Prohibited.
The Ford Edge was reported stolen from the City of Barrie on June 3, 2026.
At the time of the offence,
AGHAKHANI BARFEH
was on probation and was a prohibited driver.
Video of the incident can be seen here:
https://youtu.be/EID0w-LAINk
The ALPR system was expanded to all YRP frontline police vehicles in December 2022. The system uses a camera mounted in police vehicles to automatically scan and analyze licence plates as the officers are on patrol. This technology strengthens policing efforts by enabling officers to quickly identify stolen vehicles and respond to potential criminal activity.
Anyone with information is asked to contact the York Regional Police #1 District Criminal Investigations Bureau by calling 1-866-876-5423, ext. 7145. Anonymous tips can be sent to Crime Stoppers by calling 1-800-222-TIPS or by visiting
www.1800222tips.com
.

title: Update – Third Suspect Arrested in Vaughan Double Homicide
content:
The third suspect wanted in the double homicide in the City of Vaughan has been arrested.
On May 13, 2026, at approximately 5:30 p.m., officers attended a residence in the area of Kaiser Drive and Forest Drive after receiving reports that shots had been fired. When officers arrived, two males were located outside of the home suffering from multiple gunshot wounds. Both males succumbed to their injuries and were pronounced deceased.
While police were on scene, additional officers were dispatched to the area of Huntington Road and Kirby Road after receiving reports that a blue sport utility vehicle (SUV) had been abandoned and set on fire. Investigators have confirmed the SUV was used by the suspects leading up to and following the shooting. The vehicle was reported stolen out of the City of Toronto.
Investigative efforts led to the identification of three men who were believed to have been occupants in the same SUV.
On May 28, 2026, two men were charged in relation to the homicide.
On June 4, 2026, the third suspect, Gerard
MURRAY, 48, of the City of Windsor, was arrested in Windsor.
He is charged with two counts of First-Degree Murder and Arson Causing Property Damage.
The investigation remains ongoing.
Any witnesses who have not yet spoken to police, or anyone who may have video footage from the area is asked to come forward.
Anyone with information is asked to contact the York Regional Police Homicide Unit at 1-866-876-5423, ext. 7865, or email the Homicide Unit at
homicide@yrp.ca
. Anonymous tips can also be made to Crime Stoppers at 1-800-222-TIPS, or by visiting
www.1800222tips.com
.

title: One Charged, One Outstanding in Markham Commercial Robbery
content:
Investigators with the York Regional Police Hold-Up Unit have charged a man and are seeking the public’s assistance in identifying an additional suspect following an armed robbery in the City of Markham.
On May 14, 2026, just before 1 a.m., officers responded to reports of a robbery inside a commercial building, in the area of Warden Avenue and Masseyfield Gate. Two suspects, armed with firearms, entered the premises and stole cash and cellular phones from patrons before fleeing the scene.
There were no physical injuries.
Following a thorough investigation, on June 4, 2026, Ayden Kees
POLLMANN, 19, of the City of Toronto, was charged with Robbery with a Firearm and Disguise with Intent.
Additionally, search warrants were executed at two residences associated to
POLLMANN.
Investigators are hoping to identify the second suspect. He is described as Asian, standing at approximately 5’7” with a medium build.
Anyone with information is asked to contact the York Regional Police Hold-Up Unit at 1-866-876-5423, ext. 6630. Anonymous tips can also be made to Crime Stoppers at 1-800-222-TIPS, or by visiting
www.1800222tips.com
.

title: Charges Laid in 2022 Homicide Investigation
content:
Investigators with the York Regional Police Cold Case Section have charged a man in relation to a 2022 missing person investigation which has now been deemed a homicide.
On August 30, 2022, Roman
PERELSTEIN
was reported missing by his family. He was last seen in the area of Bathurst Street and Orchard Heights Boulevard in the Town of Aurora several weeks prior.
Before
PERELSTEIN
had been reported missing, on August 21, 2022, human remains were located along a trail in the area of Sideline 20 and Concession Road 8 in the City of Pickering. No identification could be made at the time, due to the conditions of the remains.
In March 2025, through dental records, the remains were positively identified as Roman
PERELSTEIN.
His death has been deemed a homicide.
Following a lengthy investigation, Suren
KHIZGUIIAEV, 49, of Harwich Township, was charged on May 21, 2026, with Accessory After the Fact to Murder and Indignity to a Dead Body.
The investigation is ongoing.
Anyone with information is asked to contact the York Regional Police Cold Case Section at 1-866-876-5423, ext. 6000, or email the Cold Case Section at
coldcase@yrp.ca
. Anonymous tips can also be made to Crime Stoppers at 1-800-222-TIPS, or by visiting
www.1800222tips.com
.

title: Suspect for Identification After Woman Assaulted in Vaughan
content:
Investigators with the York Regional Police Hate Crime Unit are seeking the public’s assistance in identifying a suspect after a woman was sprayed with an aerosol irritant in the City of Vaughan.
On Thursday, May 28, 2026, at approximately 7:55 p.m., officers responded to a weapons call in the area of Steeles Avenue West and New Westminster Drive. The female victim was attending a community event when she was approached by an unknown male. The suspect and victim had a brief interaction, then a short time later the suspect sprayed her with an aerosol irritant. He then fled on foot in the area of Conley Street and New Westminster Drive.
The victim was transported to hospital with non-life-threatening injuries.
The suspect is described as white, approximately 6'2", weighing 200 lbs. He was last seen wearing a black baseball hat, black sunglasses, a grey T-shirt with an Under Armour logo on the chest, black pants and black running shoes.
Investigators are releasing an image of the suspect and are asking anyone who may recognize him, or who has information, or video from the area, to come forward.
The incident is believed to be targeted. The York Regional Police Hate Crime Unit has been engaged and is working to determine whether the incident was hate-motivated.
Anyone with information is asked to contact the York Regional Police Hate Crime Prevention Unit by calling 1-866-876-5423, ext. 6552 or by email at
hatecrime@yrp.ca
. Anonymous tips can be sent to Crime Stoppers by calling 1-800-222-TIPS or by visiting
www.1800222tips.com
.

title: Man Wanted in 2025 Attempted Murder Investigation in Richmond Hill
content:
Investigators with the York Regional Police #2 District Criminal Investigations Bureau have charged two men and are seeking a third suspect in relation to an attempted murder in June 2025 in the City of Richmond Hill.
On June 21, 2025, police attended a parking lot in the area of Yonge Street and May Avenue after receiving multiple reports of an assault-in-progress. When officers arrived, a male victim was located with stab wounds resulting in serious, but non-life-threatening injuries.
Officers learned the victim was involved in a road rage incident with another male, who left the scene. Shortly after, while the victim was at a plaza, the same male returned with two other males and all three began assaulting the victim. During the assault, the victim was stabbed by one of the suspects.
One suspect was taken into custody at the scene while the two other suspects fled.
Following a lengthy investigation, a second suspect was charged on May 31, 2026.
Both suspects are facing numerous charges including attempted murder.
The third suspect, Seyed-Houman
MIRMOGHTADAEI,
of no fixed address, is currently wanted for Attempt to Commit Murder and Aggravated Assault.
He is encouraged to seek legal advice and turn himself in. Investigators are reminding the public that anyone providing assistance to him may be subject to criminal charges.
Wanted:
Seyed-Houman
MIRMOGHTADAEI, 30, of no fixed address
Charges:
Attempt to Commit Murder
Aggravated Assault
Charged:
Hamid-Reza
SAFI-POOR, 54, of the City of Toronto
Ali
KIA, 59, of the City of Markham
Attempt to Commit Murder
Aggravated Assault
Uttering Threats
Possession of a Weapon for Dangerous Purpose (
KIA
only)
Anyone with information is asked to contact the York Regional Police #2 District Criminal Investigations Bureau at 1-866-876-5423, ext. 7241. Anonymous tips can also be made to Crime Stoppers at 1-800-222-TIPS, or by visiting
www.1800222tips.com
.

title: York Regional Police Conduct Proactive Patrol to Inform Shop Owners About Counterfeit Goods
content:
York Regional Police conducted proactive patrol aimed at shops suspected of selling counterfeit goods through outreach and education.
On Thursday, June 11, 2026, members of York Regional Police attended shops in the area of Kennedy Road and Steeles Avenue East in the City of Markham to raise awareness among shop owners about the legal consequences associated with selling counterfeit goods.
Officers conducted foot patrols, spoke with vendors and distributed information cards in three languages. The initiative focused on education and prevention, including consequences of selling counterfeit trademark goods.
York Regional Police reminds vendors that police and municipal partners may conduct visible patrols and store visits in areas where counterfeit goods are suspected.
Vendors are reminded of the importance of knowing their suppliers and maintaining records to support the authenticity of the products they sell. If you sell branded goods, you must be able to show where the items came from and keep supporting documents such as invoices, receipts and shipping records.
